title: Doellingeria gormanii
taxonomy: Doellingeria gormanii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southeastern United States. The classification of Doellingeria gormanii includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its kingdom's division into the clade Angiospermophyta. Within the clade, it falls under the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Doellingeria gormanii within the subclass Asteridae.
uses: Doellingeria gormanii has been reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, there is limited scientific research available on the medicinal uses of this plant. More studies are needed to confirm its potential health benefits and safe usage. Its traditional uses may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.

intro: Doellingeria gormanii, also known as Gorman's ironweed, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Doellingeria gormanii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Doellingeria gormanii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
